headline: Warsh's debut FOMC presser hits Wednesday into a stagflation trap — 4.2% CPI, zero cut odds, and a Hormuz blockade still leaking at 15% capacity.
sub: Kevin Warsh chairs his first FOMC meeting June 16–17 with the fed funds rate at 3.50–3.75% and virtually zero probability of a cut this year (CME FedWatch). May CPI at 4.2% YoY — a 3-year high driven by a 23.5% energy surge tied to the Iran-Hormuz crisis — makes any dovish pivot impossible. Trump's June 11 'deal essentially agreed' signal partially eased crude, but visible Hormuz traffic remains at just 15% of pre-war levels with global inventories draining into summer peak demand. Retail sales land the same morning as the rate decision; Warsh's dot-plot revisions and tone on energy-driven inflation are the week's defining signals.

events.text: FOMC Decision + Warsh Debut Presser (Wed Jun 17)
events.detail: Hold at 3.50–3.75% certain; dot plot and Warsh tone on stagflation are the market movers
~2.9M bpd escaping via clandestine flows; summer peak demand risks a crude price spike
Consumer health test drops same morning as Fed decision — stagflation read

events.text: May Housing Starts & Building Permits (Tue Jun 16)
events.detail: Rate-sensitive sector; signals whether 3.50–3.75% is biting the real economy
SpaceX's $1.75T debut last week pulled Nasdaq breadth lower; watch tech rotation continue

events.text: Iran Peace Deal & Hormuz Reopening Timeline
events.detail: No formal deal signed yet; confirmed reopening is the single biggest deflationary catalyst of 2026

bias: Lean cautious/defensive — Warsh inherits a stagflation trap with CPI above 4% and no room to cut. Energy and defense remain structural longs on the Hormuz supply squeeze; tech faces multiple compression risk if the dot plot shifts hawkish. A confirmed Hormuz reopening and ceasefire would flip the tape to risk-on aggressively.
